title: Deep Work
author: Cal Newport
core_idea: Deep Work key takeaways — the whole book in one post.
bullets: Deep work (focused, cognitively demanding, no distraction) is becoming rare exactly as it becomes valuable. That's the whole thesis.
Shallow work (email, meetings, Slack) feels productive but is low-value and easy to replicate.
You have a finite amount of deep-work capacity per day (~4 hours even for experts). Protect it.
Four philosophies of scheduling deep work: monastic, bimodal, rhythmic (same block daily), journalistic. Pick one that fits your life.
Embrace boredom: if you can't tolerate a queue without your phone, you've trained your brain to crave distraction. Schedule your internet use.
honest_take: Distilled from the top 2 Reddit threads. Enable an Anthropic API key for a synthesized, opinionated take.

quotes.text: The 4-hours-of-deep-work-max claim is the single most freeing idea. Stop feeling guilty about the other 4 hours.
quotes.author: a commenter
